title: links.config.json
description: Per-machine configuration for `links`. Every field is optional; omitted fields fall back to the DEFAULTS in src/config.ts (which reproduce the original author's machine). `links init` writes a personalized starting point. Paths support a leading ~ for the home directory.

scopes.description: Ordered cwd-prefix -> scope-name rules. First match wins; the LAST entry is the fallback scope for any cwd that matches nothing earlier. Each scope becomes store/<name>/ and an MCP server suffix (links-<name>).

cwdPrefix.description: Match a session whose cwd starts with this prefix (leading ~ expands to home).

excludeProjectDirs.description: Claude Code project directory names that are synthetic corpora (e.g. claude-mem observer re-narrations) and must never be ingested.

storeDir.description: Where derived cards/index/rules live. Default: <repo>/store. Leading ~ expands to home.

sources.description: Raw, read-only per-tool session source roots. Never copied; only scanned.

extractionEngine.description: Which engine turns a transcript into a card. Only `codex` is wired today; `claude` and `api-key` are reserved for the publish step.

embedder.description: Retrieval embedder. `hash` = zero-dependency local char-n-gram (default, works on clone). `minilm` = all-MiniLM-L6-v2 via Transformers.js (~25MB one-time download, keyless/offline after) for real semantic recall on paraphrase queries.

codexBin.description: Explicit path to the codex binary, bypassing auto-discovery.

codexFallback.description: How to locate a bundled codex binary when none is on PATH and no codexBin is set. Defaults target Cursor's openai.chatgpt extension on Linux x86_64.

extensionPrefix.description: Extension dir name prefix to match. Default openai.chatgpt-.

binRelPath.description: Relative path to the codex binary inside the matched extension.

gate.description: Triviality gate thresholds — sessions below these get an index line but no card / LLM call.

junkTitleRe.description: Case-insensitive regex; a short session whose title matches is treated as a junk ask.
